'''Nichu Shrine''' (日雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at 75 Makimachi, Shigaraki, [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Koka City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]].

== {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}

== Shrine Emblem ==
* Ume Bowl (梅鉢)

== History ==
Although its founding date is unknown, the origin is traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} "'''Koka-Hikumo Shrine''' (甲可日雲宮)" recorded in {{ill|Shinto Gobu-sho|ja|神道五部書|lt=Ise Shinto Gobu-sho|lt_ja=伊勢神道五部書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Gobu-sho|1=Shintō Gobusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590920}} and the chronicle {{ill|Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131331326}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. The precincts are traversed by the [[:en:Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|Shigaraki Kogen Railway]]. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==
* Annual Festival: May 5

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], [[:en:Kagen|Kagen]] 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

== Shrine Auxiliary Facilities ==
* On-site Shrines
** {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}}
** {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}}
** {{ill|Amemiya Shrine|ja|雨宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]
* Off-site Shrines
** {{ill|Kashima Jingu|lt=Kashima Jingu|lt=Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine|qq=}}

== Access ==
* A 5‑minute walk from [[:en:Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|Shigaraki Kogen Railway]]’s [[:en:Kumoi Station|Unoi Station]]

Although its founding date is unknown, the origin is traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} "'''Koka-Hikumo Shrine''' (甲可日雲宮)" recorded in {{ill|Shinto Gobu-sho|ja|神道五部書|lt=Ise Shinto Gobu-sho|lt_ja=伊勢神道五部書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Gobu-sho|1=Shintō Gobusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590920}} and the chronicle {{ill|Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131331326}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. The precincts are traversed by the [[:en:Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|Shigaraki Kogen Railway]]. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==
* Annual Festival: May 5

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], [[:en:Kagen|Kagen]] 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

== Shrine Auxiliary Facilities ==

'''Nichu Shrine''' (日雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at 75 Makimachi, Shigaraki, [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Koka City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]].

== History ==
Although its founding date is unknown, the origin is traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} "'''Koka-Hikumo Shrine''' (甲可日雲宮)" recorded in {{ill|Shinto Gobu-sho|ja|神道五部書|lt=Ise Shinto Gobu-sho|lt_ja=伊勢神道五部書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Gobu-sho|1=Shintō Gobusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590920}} and the chronicle {{ill|Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131331326}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. The precincts are traversed by the [[:en:Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|Shigaraki Kogen Railway]]. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], [[:en:Kagen|Kagen]] 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

{{ill|Nichu Shrine|ja|日雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichu Shrine|qq=}} is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}.

Moto-Ise Shrines are [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ([[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]).

*
==Overview==
{{Infobox religious building
|名称=日雲神社
|image=File:Hikumo-jinja.JPG
|所在地=[[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Koka City]] 信楽町牧75
|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|28.4|N|136|05|00.1|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}
|社格={{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=former village shrine|lt_ja=旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
|創建=Founding date unknown
|本殿=Three-bay shrine [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]]
|例祭=[[:en:May 5|May 5]]
|神事=Taiko Dance
|prefecture={{ill|Koka|WD=Q847584}}
|coordinates={{Coord|34.907889|N|136.083361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}
}}
'''Nichu Shrine''' (日雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at 75 Makimachi, Shigaraki, [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Koka City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]].

== {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}

== Shrine Emblem ==
* Ume Bowl (梅鉢)

== History ==
Although its founding date is unknown, the origin is traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} "'''Koka-Hikumo Shrine''' (甲可日雲宮)" recorded in {{ill|Shinto Gobu-sho|ja|神道五部書|lt=Ise Shinto Gobu-sho|lt_ja=伊勢神道五部書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Gobu-sho|1=Shintō Gobusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590920}} and the chronicle {{ill|Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131331326}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. The precincts are traversed by the [[:en:Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|Shigaraki Kogen Railway]]. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==
* Annual Festival: May 5

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], [[:en:Kagen|Kagen]] 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

== Shrine Auxiliary Facilities ==
* On-site Shrines
** {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}}
** {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}}
** {{ill|Amemiya Shrine|ja|雨宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]
* Off-site Shrines
** {{ill|Kashima Jingu|lt=Kashima Jingu|lt=Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine|qq=}}

== Access ==
* A 5‑minute walk from [[:en:Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|Shigaraki Kogen Railway]]’s [[:en:Kumoi Station|Unoi Station]]

Moto-Ise Shrines are [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ([[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]).

*
==Overview==
{{Infobox religious building
|名称=日雲神社
|image=File:Hikumo-jinja.JPG
|所在地=[[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Koka City]] 信楽町牧75
|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|28.4|N|136|05|00.1|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}
|社格={{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=former village shrine|lt_ja=旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
|創建=Founding date unknown
|本殿=Three-bay shrine [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]]
|例祭=[[:en:May 5|May 5]]
|神事=Taiko Dance
|prefecture={{ill|Koka|WD=Q847584}}
|coordinates={{Coord|34.907889|N|136.083361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}

'''Nichu Shrine''' (日雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at 75 Makimachi, Shigaraki, [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Koka City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]].

== {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}

== Shrine Emblem ==
* Ume Bowl (梅鉢)

== History ==
Although its founding date is unknown, the origin is traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} "'''Koka-Hikumo Shrine''' (甲可日雲宮)" recorded in {{ill|Shinto Gobu-sho|ja|神道五部書|lt=Ise Shinto Gobu-sho|lt_ja=伊勢神道五部書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Gobu-sho|1=Shintō Gobusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590920}} and the chronicle {{ill|Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131331326}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. The precincts are traversed by the [[:en:Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|Shigaraki Kogen Railway]]. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==
* Annual Festival: May 5

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], [[:en:Kagen|Kagen]] 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

== Shrine Auxiliary Facilities ==
* On-site Shrines
** {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}}
** {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}}
** {{ill|Amemiya Shrine|ja|雨宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]
* Off-site Shrines
** {{ill|Kashima Jingu|lt=Kashima Jingu|lt=Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine|qq=}}

== Access ==
* A 5‑minute walk from [[:en:Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|Shigaraki Kogen Railway]]’s [[:en:Kumoi Station|Unoi Station]]

Although its founding date is unknown, the origin is traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} "'''Koka-Hikumo Shrine''' (甲可日雲宮)" recorded in {{ill|Shinto Gobu-sho|ja|神道五部書|lt=Ise Shinto Gobu-sho|lt_ja=伊勢神道五部書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Gobu-sho|1=Shintō Gobusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590920}} and the chronicle {{ill|Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131331326}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). The precincts are traversed by the [[:en:Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|Shigaraki Kogen Railway]]. == History ==
Although its founding date is unknown, the origin is traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} "'''Koka-Hikumo Shrine''' (甲可日雲宮)" recorded in {{ill|Shinto Gobu-sho|ja|神道五部書|lt=Ise Shinto Gobu-sho|lt_ja=伊勢神道五部書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Gobu-sho|1=Shintō Gobusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590920}} and the chronicle {{ill|Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131331326}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. The precincts are traversed by the [[:en:Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|Shigaraki Kogen Railway]]. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==
* Annual Festival: May 5

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], [[:en:Kagen|Kagen]] 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

== Shrine Auxiliary Facilities ==
* On-site Shrines
** {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}}
** {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}}
** {{ill|Amemiya Shrine|ja|雨宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]
* Off-site Shrines
** {{ill|Kashima Jingu|lt=Kashima Jingu|lt=Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine|qq=}}

'''Nichu Shrine''' (日雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at 75 Makimachi, Shigaraki, [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Koka City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]].

* Ume Bowl (梅鉢)

== History ==
Although its founding date is unknown, the origin is traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} "'''Koka-Hikumo Shrine''' (甲可日雲宮)" recorded in {{ill|Shinto Gobu-sho|ja|神道五部書|lt=Ise Shinto Gobu-sho|lt_ja=伊勢神道五部書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Gobu-sho|1=Shintō Gobusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590920}} and the chronicle {{ill|Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131331326}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. The precincts are traversed by the [[:en:Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|Shigaraki Kogen Railway]]. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==
* Annual Festival: May 5

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], [[:en:Kagen|Kagen]] 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

{{ill|Nichu Shrine|ja|日雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichu Shrine|qq=}} is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}.

Moto-Ise Shrines are [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ([[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]).

*
==Overview==
{{Infobox religious building
|名称=日雲神社
|image=File:Hikumo-jinja.JPG
|所在地=[[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Koka City]] 信楽町牧75
|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|28.4|N|136|05|00.1|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}
|社格={{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=former village shrine|lt_ja=旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
|創建=Founding date unknown
|本殿=Three-bay shrine [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]]
|例祭=[[:en:May 5|May 5]]
|神事=Taiko Dance
|prefecture={{ill|Koka|WD=Q847584}}
|coordinates={{Coord|34.907889|N|136.083361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}
}}
'''Nichu Shrine''' (日雲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at 75 Makimachi, Shigaraki, [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Koka City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]].

== {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}

== Shrine Emblem ==
* Ume Bowl (梅鉢)

== History ==
Although its founding date is unknown, the origin is traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} "'''Koka-Hikumo Shrine''' (甲可日雲宮)" recorded in {{ill|Shinto Gobu-sho|ja|神道五部書|lt=Ise Shinto Gobu-sho|lt_ja=伊勢神道五部書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Gobu-sho|1=Shintō Gobusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590920}} and the chronicle {{ill|Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131331326}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. The precincts are traversed by the [[:en:Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|Shigaraki Kogen Railway]]. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==
* Annual Festival: May 5

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], [[:en:Kagen|Kagen]] 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

== Shrine Auxiliary Facilities ==
* On-site Shrines
** {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}}
** {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}}
** {{ill|Amemiya Shrine|ja|雨宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]
* Off-site Shrines
** {{ill|Kashima Jingu|lt=Kashima Jingu|lt=Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine|qq=}}

== Access ==
* A 5‑minute walk from [[:en:Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|Shigaraki Kogen Railway]]’s [[:en:Kumoi Station|Unoi Station]]

It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>

{{ill|Nichu Shrine|ja|日雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichu Shrine|qq=}} is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}.

Moto-Ise Shrines are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrines}} that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingu}}. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine}}).

*
==Overview==
{{Infobox religious building
|名称=日雲神社
|image=File:Hikumo-jinja.JPG
|所在地={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Koka City}} 信楽町牧75
|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|28.4|N|136|05|00.1|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}
|社格={{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=former village shrine|lt_ja=旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
|創建=Founding date unknown
|本殿=Three-bay shrine {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}}
|例祭={{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5}}
|神事=Taiko Dance
|prefecture={{ill|Koka|WD=Q847584}}
|coordinates={{Coord|34.907889|N|136.083361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}
}}
'''Nichu Shrine''' (日雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located at 75 Makimachi, Shigaraki, {{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Koka City}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}

== Shrine Emblem ==
* Ume Bowl (梅鉢)

== History ==
Although its founding date is unknown, the origin is traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} "'''Koka-Hikumo Shrine''' (甲可日雲宮)" recorded in {{ill|Shinto Gobu-sho|ja|神道五部書|lt=Ise Shinto Gobu-sho|lt_ja=伊勢神道五部書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Gobu-sho|1=Shintō Gobusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590920}} and the chronicle {{ill|Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131331326}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 4 (1691). It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. The precincts are traversed by the {{ill|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|en|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|WD=Q7496306|lt=Shigaraki Kogen Railway}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==
* Annual Festival: May 5

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kagen|en|Kagen|WD=Q1327409|lt=Kagen}} 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

== Shrine Auxiliary Facilities ==
* On-site Shrines
** {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}}
** {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}}
** {{ill|Amemiya Shrine|ja|雨宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine}}
* Off-site Shrines
** {{ill|Kashima Jingū|lt=Kashima Jingū|lt=Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine|qq=}}

== Access ==
* A 5‑minute walk from {{ill|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|en|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|WD=Q7496306|lt=Shigaraki Kogen Railway}}’s {{ill|Kumoi Station|en|Kumoi Station|WD=Q11659999|lt=Unoi Station}}

|所在地={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Koka City}} 信楽町牧75
|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|28.4|N|136|05|00.1|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}
|社格={{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=former village shrine|lt_ja=旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
|創建=Founding date unknown
|本殿=Three-bay shrine {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}}
|例祭={{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5}}
|神事=Taiko Dance
|prefecture={{ill|Koka|WD=Q847584}}
|coordinates={{Coord|34.907889|N|136.083361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}

'''Nichu Shrine''' (日雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located at 75 Makimachi, Shigaraki, {{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Koka City}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}

== Shrine Emblem ==
* Ume Bowl (梅鉢)

== History ==
Although its founding date is unknown, the origin is traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} "'''Koka-Hikumo Shrine''' (甲可日雲宮)" recorded in {{ill|Shinto Gobu-sho|ja|神道五部書|lt=Ise Shinto Gobu-sho|lt_ja=伊勢神道五部書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Gobu-sho|1=Shintō Gobusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590920}} and the chronicle {{ill|Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131331326}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 4 (1691). It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. The precincts are traversed by the {{ill|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|en|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|WD=Q7496306|lt=Shigaraki Kogen Railway}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==
* Annual Festival: May 5

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kagen|en|Kagen|WD=Q1327409|lt=Kagen}} 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

== Shrine Auxiliary Facilities ==
* On-site Shrines
** {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}}
** {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}}
** {{ill|Amemiya Shrine|ja|雨宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine}}
* Off-site Shrines
** {{ill|Kashima Jingū|lt=Kashima Jingū|lt=Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine|qq=}}

== Access ==
* A 5‑minute walk from {{ill|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|en|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|WD=Q7496306|lt=Shigaraki Kogen Railway}}’s {{ill|Kumoi Station|en|Kumoi Station|WD=Q11659999|lt=Unoi Station}}

It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. The precincts are traversed by the {{ill|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|en|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|WD=Q7496306|lt=Shigaraki Kogen Railway}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==
* Annual Festival: May 5

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kagen|en|Kagen|WD=Q1327409|lt=Kagen}} 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

== Shrine Auxiliary Facilities ==
* On-site Shrines

Moto-Ise Shrines are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrines}} that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingu}}. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine}}).

'''Nichu Shrine''' (日雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located at 75 Makimachi, Shigaraki, {{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Koka City}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}

== Shrine Emblem ==
* Ume Bowl (梅鉢)

== History ==
Although its founding date is unknown, the origin is traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} "'''Koka-Hikumo Shrine''' (甲可日雲宮)" recorded in {{ill|Shinto Gobu-sho|ja|神道五部書|lt=Ise Shinto Gobu-sho|lt_ja=伊勢神道五部書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Gobu-sho|1=Shintō Gobusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590920}} and the chronicle {{ill|Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131331326}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 4 (1691). It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. The precincts are traversed by the {{ill|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|en|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|WD=Q7496306|lt=Shigaraki Kogen Railway}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==
* Annual Festival: May 5

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kagen|en|Kagen|WD=Q1327409|lt=Kagen}} 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

{{ill|Nichu Shrine|ja|日雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichu Shrine|qq=}} is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}.

Moto-Ise Shrines are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrines}} that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingu}}. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine}}).

*
==Overview==
{{Infobox religious building
|名称=日雲神社
|image=File:Hikumo-jinja.JPG
|所在地={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Koka City}} 信楽町牧75
|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|28.4|N|136|05|00.1|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}
|社格={{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=former village shrine|lt_ja=旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
|創建=Founding date unknown
|本殿=Three-bay shrine {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}}
|例祭={{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5}}
|神事=Taiko Dance
|prefecture={{ill|Koka|WD=Q847584}}
|coordinates={{Coord|34.907889|N|136.083361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}
}}
'''Nichu Shrine''' (日雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located at 75 Makimachi, Shigaraki, {{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Koka City}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}

== Shrine Emblem ==
* Ume Bowl (梅鉢)

== History ==
Although its founding date is unknown, the origin is traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} "'''Koka-Hikumo Shrine''' (甲可日雲宮)" recorded in {{ill|Shinto Gobu-sho|ja|神道五部書|lt=Ise Shinto Gobu-sho|lt_ja=伊勢神道五部書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Gobu-sho|1=Shintō Gobusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590920}} and the chronicle {{ill|Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131331326}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 4 (1691). It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. The precincts are traversed by the {{ill|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|en|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|WD=Q7496306|lt=Shigaraki Kogen Railway}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==
* Annual Festival: May 5

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kagen|en|Kagen|WD=Q1327409|lt=Kagen}} 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

== Shrine Auxiliary Facilities ==
* On-site Shrines
** {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}}
** {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}}
** {{ill|Amemiya Shrine|ja|雨宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine}}
* Off-site Shrines
** {{ill|Kashima Jingū|lt=Kashima Jingū|lt=Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine|qq=}}

== Access ==
* A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|en|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|WD=Q7496306|lt=Shigaraki Kogen Railway}}’s {{ill|Kumoi Station|en|Kumoi Station|WD=Q11659999|lt=Unoi Station}}

{{ill|Nichu Shrine|ja|日雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichu Shrine|qq=}} is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}.

Moto-Ise Shrines are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingu|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}).

*
==Overview==
{{Infobox religious building
|名称=日雲神社
|image=File:Hikumo-jinja.JPG
|所在地={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Koka City|1=Kōka, Shiga}} 信楽町牧75
|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|28.4|N|136|05|00.1|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}
|社格={{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=former village shrine|lt_ja=旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
|創建=Founding date unknown
|本殿=Three-bay shrine {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
|例祭={{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5|1=May 5}}
|神事=Taiko Dance
|prefecture={{ill|Koka|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}
|coordinates={{Coord|34.907889|N|136.083361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}
}}
'''Nichu Shrine''' (日雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located at 75 Makimachi, Shigaraki, {{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Koka City|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}

== Shrine Emblem ==
* Ume Bowl (梅鉢)

== History ==
Although its founding date is unknown, the origin is traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} "'''Koka-Hikumo Shrine''' (甲可日雲宮)" recorded in {{ill|Shinto Gobu-sho|ja|神道五部書|lt=Ise Shinto Gobu-sho|lt_ja=伊勢神道五部書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Gobu-sho|1=Shintō Gobusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590920}} and the chronicle {{ill|Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131331326}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 4 (1691). It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. The precincts are traversed by the {{ill|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|en|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|WD=Q7496306|lt=Shigaraki Kogen Railway|1=Shigaraki Kohgen Railway}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==
* Annual Festival: May 5

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kagen|en|Kagen|WD=Q1327409|lt=Kagen|1=Kagen}} 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

== Shrine Auxiliary Facilities ==
* On-site Shrines
** {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}}
** {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}}
** {{ill|Amemiya Shrine|ja|雨宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}}
* Off-site Shrines
** {{ill|Kashima Jingū|lt=Kashima Jingū|lt=Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine|qq=}}

== Access ==
* A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|en|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|WD=Q7496306|lt=Shigaraki Kogen Railway|1=Shigaraki Kohgen Railway}}’s {{ill|Kumoi Station|en|Kumoi Station|WD=Q11659999|lt=Unoi Station|1=Kumoi Station}}

|神事=Taiko Dance
|prefecture={{ill|Koka|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}
|coordinates={{Coord|34.907889|N|136.083361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}

'''Nichu Shrine''' (日雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located at 75 Makimachi, Shigaraki, {{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Koka City|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}

== Shrine Emblem ==
* Ume Bowl (梅鉢)

== History ==
Although its founding date is unknown, the origin is traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} "'''Koka-Hikumo Shrine''' (甲可日雲宮)" recorded in {{ill|Shinto Gobu-sho|ja|神道五部書|lt=Ise Shinto Gobu-sho|lt_ja=伊勢神道五部書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Gobu-sho|1=Shintō Gobusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590920}} and the chronicle {{ill|Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131331326}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 4 (1691). It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. The precincts are traversed by the {{ill|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|en|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|WD=Q7496306|lt=Shigaraki Kogen Railway|1=Shigaraki Kohgen Railway}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==
* Annual Festival: May 5

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kagen|en|Kagen|WD=Q1327409|lt=Kagen|1=Kagen}} 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

== Shrine Auxiliary Facilities ==
* On-site Shrines
** {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}}
** {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}}
** {{ill|Amemiya Shrine|ja|雨宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}}
* Off-site Shrines
** {{ill|Kashima Jingū|lt=Kashima Jingū|lt=Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine|qq=}}

== Access ==
* A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|en|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|WD=Q7496306|lt=Shigaraki Kogen Railway|1=Shigaraki Kohgen Railway}}’s {{ill|Kumoi Station|en|Kumoi Station|WD=Q11659999|lt=Unoi Station|1=Kumoi Station}}

The precincts are traversed by the {{ill|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|en|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|WD=Q7496306|lt=Shigaraki Kogen Railway|1=Shigaraki Kohgen Railway}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==
* Annual Festival: May 5

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kagen|en|Kagen|WD=Q1327409|lt=Kagen|1=Kagen}} 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

== Shrine Auxiliary Facilities ==
* On-site Shrines
** {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}}

 and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the EverybodyWiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Nichu Shrine|ja|日雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichu Shrine|qq=}} is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}.

Moto-Ise Shrines are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingu|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}).

*
==Overview==
{{Infobox religious building
|名称=日雲神社
|image=File:Hikumo-jinja.JPG
|所在地={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Koka City|1=Kōka, Shiga}} 信楽町牧75
|位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|28.4|N|136|05|00.1|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}
|社格={{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=former village shrine|lt_ja=旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}

'''Nichu Shrine''' (日雲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located at 75 Makimachi, Shigaraki, {{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Koka City|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}

== Shrine Emblem ==
* Ume Bowl (梅鉢)

== History ==
Although its founding date is unknown, the origin is traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} "'''Koka-Hikumo Shrine''' (甲可日雲宮)" recorded in {{ill|Shinto Gobu-sho|ja|神道五部書|lt=Ise Shinto Gobu-sho|lt_ja=伊勢神道五部書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Gobu-sho|1=Shintō Gobusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590920}} and the chronicle {{ill|Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Hime no Mikoto Chronicles|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131331326}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 4 (1691). It was once known as "Uenoyama Tenjin" and "Makimura Tenmangu", but in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 18 (1885) its name was changed to the current one. The precincts are traversed by the {{ill|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|en|Shigaraki Kohgen Railway|WD=Q7496306|lt=Shigaraki Kogen Railway|1=Shigaraki Kohgen Railway}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 9 (1876) it was listed as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Festivals ==
* Annual Festival: May 5

== Cultural Properties ==
* Main Hall – A three-bay shrine structure in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style with a cypress bark roof {{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}, said to have been constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 4 (1691). It is registered as a National {{ill|Registered tangible cultural property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered tangible cultural property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} as a representative example of the Nagare-zukuri tradition in Ōmi since the medieval period.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Koka City History Vol.8 Koka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Koka City}}</ref>
* Hexagonal Stone Lantern – Possessing an inscription from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kagen|en|Kagen|WD=Q1327409|lt=Kagen|1=Kagen}} 4 (1306), designated as a cultural property by Koka City.
* Taiko Dance – Selected intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} of Shiga Prefecture.

== Shrine Auxiliary Facilities ==
* On-site Shrines

** {{ill|Amemiya Shrine|ja|雨宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
